explanatory_strength.rationale: Round-two review does not upgrade the mechanism to quality reinvestment: FY2026 revenue ¥22.5B, AUM ¥1.34tn, base fees +17.1% and success fees +53.6% (~65% of combined fees), mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh versus FY2026 peak ¥317.33, and explicit cycle normalization remain a croupier fee-on-AUM story. Archetype is croupier not compounder, moat partial not stable/widening, holdco profit flows through subsidiaries, and the etf_product_pipeline row still uses owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf without a reinvestment rate or incremental ROIC bridge required by the quality_reinvestment route.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: The 2026-07-24 contract backfill closes the economic ownership map: all four component calculation proofs are mechanically valid, overlap keys are unique, and filing_facts reconcile revenue, EPS, AUM, fee split, BVPS ¥757.01, and 27.12M post-split shares. Decision-grade gaps from round one persist: no formal targeted research_response was delivered; live_price_confirmed remains false (Yahoo ¥757 versus last exchange print ¥464 on 100 shares, with ¥757 matching BVPS ¥757.01); yuho not mirrored locally; management_facts claims are unverified; etf_product_pipeline still terminates at judgment pipeline_value_per_share (¥40–220/sh) with no reinvestment rate, incremental ROIC, or distributable-cash runway.

downside_control.rationale: Asset-light balance sheet limits insolvency risk, and the contract low case still implies ¥1,176/sh versus ¥757 price (6.49% annualized). Permanent loss is not structurally bounded at spot: earnings are ~65% performance-fee exposed and market-beta, there is no book floor for owner cash, TSE tape is non-continuous (two nonzero-volume days in twelve months), and the performance-fee cycle reserve (base −¥140/sh) only partially captures peak-cycle risk. Execution risk can overwhelm theoretical upside if the Yahoo quote is not an investable market price.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return 16.94% at ¥757 clears the 12% Buffett/Weschler hurdle on paper (low 6.49%, high 26.83%), and component base ¥2,264/sh implies ~199% upside to that price. Those paths depend on an unconfirmed quote that may be BVPS mislabeled, mid-cycle normalization holding, and judgment-heavy reinvestment and book-claim rows. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% and thesis 53.33% at ¥464 are not executable on public feeds. Return is attractive only if price and liquidity are verified; conservative owner-earnings reinvestment proof remains incomplete.

claims.claim: Simplex Financial Holdings remains outside the classic Buffett/Weschler power zone: croupier archetype, partial moat, partial dhando, and cyclical success-fee earnings. The 2026-07-24 contract backfill improves mechanical completeness but does not convert the name into a durable compounder with proven reinvestable owner earnings.

Mid-cycle owner cash of ¥137.14/sh is the correct croupier anchor versus FY2026 peak ¥317.33, and the core_fee_engine proof reconciles to ¥2,239/sh base with valid overlap controls, but the etf_product_pipeline reinvestment row still lacks incremental ROIC or reinvestment-rate traces required by the quality_reinvestment route.

At unconfirmed ¥757, contract base return 16.94% per year may clear the 12% hurdle on paper, but illiquid TSE execution, peak success-fee cyclicality, and absent targeted research on live quote and reinvestment economics block approve; watch pending broker-confirmed price, liquidity, and filing-verified reinvestment path.

strongest_counter_explanation: Japan's structural ETF adoption and retail equity re-rating make Simplex's Japan-first active and thematic pipeline a widening-moat fee compounder: FY2026 success fees reflect sustainable alpha and product pull-through rather than a cyclical peak, illiquidity permanently misprices the float, and patient owners will capture component value (~¥2,264/sh) as base fees compound with AUM regardless of short-term performance-fee normalization.
most_important_missing_fact: Live TSE quote with depth plus filing-verified reinvestment rate and incremental return on capital on retained owner cash, with parent-to-subsidiary dividend flow reconciled in yuho to separate durable base-fee owner earnings from cyclical success-fee earnings.
falsifiers: Normalized owner cash per share falls below ¥100 for two consecutive fiscal years without a disclosed structural fee cut.
Success fees decline more than 30% year over year for two consecutive quarters with no offsetting base-fee or AUM growth.
Incremental return on capital on disclosed reinvestment stays below 12% while payout exceeds 80% of distributable owner cash.
TSE shows zero reported volume for two consecutive quarters with no disclosed block trade or liquidity program.
specialist_findings: Outside power zone (croupier archetype, moat partial, dhando partial; Buffett rules require compounder/holding_co/serial_acquirer/infrastructure plus stable/widening moat — only financial_services sleeve matches). Round-two re-audit after 2026-07-24 contract backfill: all four valuation-proof rows mechanically valid with empty double_counting_flags; additive base sum ¥2,264.38/sh = ¥2,239.37 + ¥100.00 + ¥65.03 − ¥140.02; economic ownership map and overlap control met per evidence_reconciliation. etf_product_pipeline uses method_id owner_earnings_reinvestment_dcf but trace stops at judgment pipeline_value_per_share with filing facts (AUM, revenue, shares) only — fails route required_evidence on reinvestment rate and incremental ROIC. net_financial_claims applies 0–17.2% claim_share haircut on BVPS for holdco pass-through (wide judgment). performance_fee_cycle_reserve quantifies peak-cycle reserve (~1.02× mid-cycle owner cash at base). No comparables attached. Formal targeted research_response.json absent at packet freeze; round-one missing facts on live quote and reinvestment economics remain unresolved. live_price_confirmed false; Yahoo ¥757 equals BVPS ¥757.01 from finfo2026 — likely vendor mislabeling versus last print ¥464 (100 shares, Jan 2026). Contract base annualized return 16.94% at ¥757 vs 12% hurdle; Lawrence synthesis 38.04% diverges from executive-summary ¥464 framing (53.33%) flagged by Milly. Dividend policy: no cash dividend FY2025–FY2026; reinvestment without ROIC proof. Capital requirement risk is earnings cyclicality and liquidity, not balance-sheet insolvency.
